item.title: Pernod Ricard Weighs Deal for Jack Daniel’s Maker Brown-Forman
item.description: Pernod Ricard SA is exploring a potential acquisition of Brown-Forman Corp., the owner of Jack Daniel’s whiskey, as alcoholic drink companies look at ways to consolidate amid an industry downturn, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: How a Social Media Trial Verdict Threatens Big Tech
item.description: A flood of lawsuits filed in recent years against social media giants claim that by engineering their platforms to be hard to resist, the companies are causing serious harm to young users. In the first case to go to trial, a jury on March 25 found Meta Platforms Inc. and Google negligent in the design and operation of their platforms – a landmark verdict.

item.title: BNP Paribas Set to Roll Out AI Companion for All Staff
item.description: BNP Paribas SA is set to deploy an artificial intelligence companion for all staff as Chief Executive Officer Jean-Laurent Bonnafe bets the technology can lower costs across the bank.

item.title: Mortgage Rates Jump to 6.38% as War Rattles Housing Market
item.description: US mortgage rates jumped for a fourth straight week, reaching the highest point in six months and dampening prospects for the crucial spring season as the Iran war roils markets.

item.title: Turkey’s $8 Billion Gold Drawdown Since Iran War Hits Bullion
item.description: Turkey’s central bank sold and swapped about 60 tons of gold, worth more than $8 billion, in two weeks after the start of the war in Iran, adding to downward pressure on bullion prices.

item.title: Amazon Offers $1,000 Prizes for Drivers Who Say Why They Love Their Job
item.description: Amazon.com Inc., the world’s largest e-commerce company, is offering $1,000 prizes for delivery drivers who share stories about why they love their jobs.

item.title: Wall Street Groups Urge Judge to Toss Optimum Antitrust Suit
item.description: Wall Street trade groups lined up behind Apollo Global Management and other firms seeking dismissal of a lawsuit by Optimum Communications Inc. accusing lenders of forming an “illegal cartel” to freeze the telecom company out of the US credit market.

item.title: Lego Billionaires’ Family Office Hit by Poor Investment Returns
item.description: Profits dropped at the family office of the billionaire owners of Lego last year after disappointing returns in its investment arm offset a standout year at the toymaker.

item.title: Musk’s X Loses Suit Claiming Advertiser Boycott of Platform
item.description: The World Federation of Advertisers, Nestle SA, Shell Plc and a handful of other companies won dismissal of a lawsuit filed by Elon Musk’s X over claims that they orchestrated an advertising boycott of the social media platform after the billionaire purchased it.

item.title: FCC Strikes Copper Retirement Rules, Speeding Fiber Transition
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission voted Thursday to phase out regulations that made it harder for phone and internet service providers to swap out copper communications networks for fiber optic lines.

item.title: European Missile Maker MBDA Sees Rising Demand Amid Iran War
item.description: MBDA, Europe’s biggest missile manufacturer, plans to expand production by 40% this year and work on interceptors, saying demand has surged since the outbreak of the Iran war.

item.title: Private Credit Funds Trap $5 Billion as Investors Rush for Exit
item.description: A wave of redemption requests across the private credit industry has left more than $4.6 billion of investor capital trapped behind withdrawal limits, with more asset managers expected to impose curbs in the coming weeks.

item.title: Costa Rica Colon Hits Two-Decade High, Pressuring Central Bank
item.description: Costa Rica’s colon surged to its strongest level in more than two decades on a wave of exports and foreign investment, leading the central bank to step up interventions in the currency market.

item.title: US Regulator Chides Big Tech for Lack of Engagement on Grids
item.description: The chairman of the US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission said big tech companies seeking more power for their data centers don’t engage enough with her agency.

item.title: Clear Secure Analyst Says Firm Emerging as Winner From TSA Chaos
item.description: While record-breaking airport wait times may be frustrating travelers and disrupting vacation plans across the country, the chaos has been a boon for security firm Clear Secure Inc., which has been counting on desperate passengers to boost to its membership numbers, according to analysts at DA Davidson & Co.

item.title: Saudi Wealth Fund Commits to Global Investments Despite Iran War
item.description: The top official at Saudi Arabia’s wealth fund said it remains committed to investments around the world despite growing concerns over the mounting economic costs of the war.

item.title: Credit Problems Jump After Betting Is Legalized, Fed Study Finds
item.description: US states that have legalized sports betting have seen a rise in delinquency rates on consumer credit products and a decrease in credit scores, according to a new report from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

item.title: Bonds Fall as Oil Resumes Rise and Auction Faces Balky Investors
item.description: Treasuries fell amid a global government bond selloff stoked by rising oil prices as US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with an escalation in American military action.

item.title: Poland Plans Fuel Tax Cuts to Shield Consumers From High Prices
item.description: Poland is joining a group of countries that shield consumers from surging fuel prices with a plan to cut taxes and cap prices at the pump, according to Prime Minister Donald Tusk.

item.title: Back From Venezuela, Veteran Investor Calls Its Debt a Top Bet
item.description: Veteran investor Arif Joshi flew to Caracas this week to get a read on where Venezuela is heading after the end of a 13-year presidency that left it in default on its debt and isolated from the global economy.

item.title: Ghana to Raise Seven-Year Debt in First Cedi Bond Since Default
item.description: Ghana will sell its first local-currency bond next week for the first time since its 2022 debt default, returning to the market to help finance its budget.

item.title: Key Russian Oil Port Resumes Loading Amid Attempt to Divert Flow
item.description: Russia’s top oil port in the Baltic Sea resumed loading days after it came under attack from Ukrainian drones, although the company that pipes crude there said it is trying to divert barrels elsewhere because of the incidents.

item.title: Crypto Enters the Mortgage Market Via Fannie Mae-Eligible Loans
item.description: Crypto is moving deeper into the US financial system — this time through the mortgage market.

item.title: South Africa Holds Rates Over Iran War Inflation Concerns
item.description: South Africa’s central bank held interest rates steady to assess the impact of the global oil-price shock caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Data-Center Shift From Middle East Would Test US Grid
item.description: Network operators are already scrambling to add capacity as demand from the tech industry soars.

item.title: Sonos’ New Play Speaker Shows the Company is Finally Back on Track
item.description: Sonos Inc. is back doing what it does best: putting out high-quality Wi-Fi speakers that look nice, sound good and work best together as a system.

item.title: Bertelsmann Profits Down With Declining Results of TV unit RTL
item.description: The outgoing chief executive officer of Germany’s biggest media company said the TV and streaming industries in Europe is facing a crisis and must consolidate.

item.title: Defense Startup Shield AI Nabs $2 Billion at $12.7 Billion Value
item.description: Shield AI has raised $2 billion at a valuation of $12.7 billion, more than doubling its value from a year ago, underscoring investor demand for defense technology and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Barrick Slows Pakistan Copper Project Amid Rising Conflicts
item.description: Barrick Mining Corp. said it’s slowing work on its Reko Diq copper-and-gold project in Pakistan due to escalating security issues in the country and Middle East.

item.title: US Continuing Jobless Claims Drop to Lowest in Almost Two Years
item.description: Recurring applications for US unemployment benefits fell to the lowest level in almost two years, indicating ongoing stability in the labor market despite fresh economic headwinds.
